items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Live from Prayagraj as millions of Hindu devotees gather in the north Indian state of Uttar Pradesh for the Maha Kumbh festival, said to be the largest religious congregation on Earth.

Nearly 400 million people are expected to participate in the festival between Jan.13 and Feb.26 for a series of ritual baths at the confluence of the Ganges, the Yamuna and the mythical Saraswati rivers. Hindu faithful believe the holy dip will cleanse them of their past sins and end their process of reincarnation. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3DT6gGq

#india #hindu #mahakumbh2025

Live from Prayagraj as millions of Hindu devotees gather in the north Indian state of Uttar Pradesh for the Maha Kumbh festival, said to be the largest religious congregation on earth.

Nearly 400 million people are expected to participate in the festival between Jan. 13 and Feb. 26 for a series of ritual baths at the confluence of the Ganges, the Yamuna and the mythical Saraswati rivers. Hindu faithful believe the holy dip will cleanse them of their past sins and end their process of reincarnation.

#india #kumbh #live

items.title: A start-up city in Kenya tries to tackle Africa's problem of urbanizing while poor
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Tatu City outside Kenya's capital is a start-up city, privately owned and with aspirations of eventually having a population of 250,000. There are similar projects around the world. But in sub-Saharan Africa, champions hope that new-city developments can address the continent’s urbanization conundrum: While the growth of cities has rolled back poverty across the world, the region has largely been an exception. Sub-Saharan Africa is urbanizing while still poor. The region lacks the capital needed to transform cities into engines of economic growth. Some experts consider Tatu City with its new infrastructure and strict rules to be a model. (AP video by Jackson Njehia)

items.title: LIVE: Watch an albatross couple share egg duty in New Zealand
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Credit: New Zealand Department of Conservation and Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Watch live as a pair of royal albatrosses shares egg duty in New Zealand. The couple are stars of Royal Cam, a 24-hour livestream of the birds’ breeding season at Taiaroa Head — a rugged headland on New Zealand’s South Island. The stream, now in its 10th season, was established to raise awareness of the vulnerable species, numbers of which have grown slowly over decades of painstaking conservation measures. Millions have watched the stream since it began in 2016, but its popularity exploded during the coronavirus pandemic.

#albatross #eggcam #royalcam #newzealand #live

items.title: Jimmy Carter funeral LIVE: Trump, Biden, Obama, Clinton, Bush in attendance
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Watch live coverage of former President Jimmy Carter’s state funeral in Washington, D.C., hosted by AP journalist Bill Barrow. 

Carter will be transported from the U.S. Capitol to the Washington National Cathedral where President Joe Biden will deliver a eulogy. Other living former presidents, including President-elect Donald Trump, are expected to attend.

After the funeral, Air Force One will carry Carter and his family back to Georgia. Carter will be buried next to his wife, former first lady Rosalynn Carter, in a plot near the home they built in Plains, Georgia. Follow live updates: https://bit.ly/3PriI2T

#jimmycarter #funeral #washington #usa #live #news

items.title: Parents in Africa struggle with unpredictable school fees that force children to drop out
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
The daily struggle to pay often unpredictable and unregulated school fees is a crushing issue for many across sub-Saharan Africa, where the lack of a few hundred dollars can determine a child’s future. The region has long had the world’s highest dropout rates. Reasons vary, but financial pain is the biggest. The World Bank found that parents across the region rank school fees more of a challenge than medical bills and other expenses. Even government-run schools can routinely bar students. In Uganda, parents describe constant phone calls from schools demanding money and making threats. (AP Video by Patrick Onen. Production by Jackson Njehia). Read more: https://bit.ly/4265hN1 

#africa #education

items.title: Two babies among the dead at Gaza hospital after Israeli airstrikes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Israeli airstrikes killed at least nine people in the Gaza Strip on Wednesday, including four infants and two women, according to Palestinian medics. Late Wednesday, the bodies of five people and eight wounded arrived at Al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital after an Israeli airstrike in Deir al-Balah. Among the dead were two babies, including a 1-week-old infant, as well as a child and a woman. The other infant's age could not immediately be determined because of how badly they were burned, an AP journalist reported. (AP Video by Abdal Kareem Hana / Production by Wafaa Shurafa) Read more here: https://bit.ly/3Wb2E9f

items.title: AI helps Israeli journalist, who has ALS, return to onscreen reporting
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
A veteran Israeli journalist who has the degenerative disease ALS is to return to onscreen reporting thanks to an AI program that will recreate his voice. Moshe Nussbaum will write and type his lines into a program, which will be read using an AI program trained to speak using his voice. He will then be filmed as if he were presenting and his lips will be “technologically adjusted” to the words.

items.title: Trump and Greenland: Why is the island coveted? AP Explains
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Remote, icy and mostly pristine, Greenland plays an outsized role in the daily weather experienced by billions of people and in the climate changes taking shape all over the planet. Since his first term in office, President-elect Donald Trump has expressed interest in acquiring Greenland, which is a semiautonomous territory of Denmark, a longtime U.S. ally and a founding member of NATO. It is also home to a large U.S. military base. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3DMT5qu

#trump #greenland #news

items.title: LIVE: View of fires in Los Angeles, California
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Live from Los Angeles as firefighters scrambled to corral a fast-moving wildfire as a potentially “life-threatening, destructive” windstorm hit Southern California on Tuesday.

Fire crews responded Tuesday morning to a handful of small blazes across the region, including one that prompted evacuation orders for neighborhoods of large homes in the foothills of the Pacific Palisades area in western Los Angeles. 

The Palisades Fire swiftly consumed more than 200 acres (81 hectares) of dry brush and sent up a huge plume of smoke visible across the city.

#california #fire #news #live #losangeles

items.title: Denmark's PM responds to Trump refusing to rule out taking control of Greenland
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Addressing Trump's comments in an interview with Danish broadcaster TV2, Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen said she did not believe that the United States will use military or economic power to secure control over Greenland.

Frederiksen repeated that she welcomed the U.S. taking a greater interest in the Arctic region, but that it would have to be done in a way that is "respectful" of the Greenland population. Read more: https://bit.ly/4fNkINq

items.content:

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
The eldest son of Donald Trump on Tuesday visited Greenland, the mineral-rich Danish territory that’s home to a large U.S. military base. The US president-elect told reporters later in the day that he wouldn’t rule out using military force to take control of Greenland. That comment, and the visit, heightened speculation that the incoming U.S. administration could seek to acquire the strategic territory in the Arctic. Read more: https://bit.ly/3W3Renl

items.title: Syrians describe horrors of Assad's system of detention, torture and death
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
As tens of thousands disappeared over more than a decade, a blanket of fear kept the Syrian population silent. People rarely told anyone that a loved one had vanished for fear they too could be reported to security agencies. Now, everyone is talking. The Associated Press visited over half a dozen of these facilities in Damascus and spoke to nine former detainees, some released on day of Assad was ousted. (AP video and production by Malak Harb) Read more here: https://bit.ly/40993CO

items.content:

At least 8 inches of snow were expected, particularly north of Interstate 70, as the National Weather Service issued winter storm warnings for Kansas and Missouri, where blizzard conditions were reported. items.title: Israel's Katz threatens ceasefire collapse if Lebanese army not deployed south of Litani river

items.content:

items.title: Venezuelan opposition leader and Argentine President Milei greet crowds in Buenos Aires
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
The former Venezuelan opposition candidate Edmundo González, who claims to have won the election against Nicolás Maduro and is recognized by some countries as the legitimate president-elect of Venezuela, returned to Latin America from his exile in Madrid to meet with the president of Argentina on Saturday, just days before the inauguration of the new leader. González appeared publicly in Buenos Aires on Saturday in front of hundreds of supporters following a meeting at the Casa Rosada with Argentine President Javier Milei, his ally. This marks the beginning of what he called a Latin American tour. (AP Video/Cristian Kovadloff) Read more here: https://bit.ly/4gZKzCU

items.content:

items.title: Jimmy Carter funeral: LIVE state service in Georgia
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Watch live as funeral services begin Saturday in Georgia for former President Jimmy Carter.
The state funeral begins with the arrival of the Carter family at Phoebe Sumter Medical Center in Americus, Georgia. The motorcade then travels through Carter’s hometown of Plains, Georgia, for a brief pause in front of his family’s farm.
The motorcade will stop at Georgia’s State Capitol for a moment of silence before Carter’s remains are transported to the Carter Presidential Center for an arrival ceremony and service. Following the service, he will lie in repose for mourners to pay their respects.

items.content:

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

Women struggle to dress modestly while crowded into a tent with extended family members, including men, and with strangers only steps away in neighbouring tents. items.title: The butterfly collector with more than 4.2 million insects seeks a safe haven
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Steve Collins started collecting butterflies more than 60 years ago. He now has a collection of 4.2 million butterflies that cuts across 900 species. From the largest known species ever discovered to the smallest butterfly, he captures and preserves them in glass covered frames that are neatly stacked in shelves to keep them away from light which could fade their beautiful colour. His biggest fear is that his noble cause might not continue, as he can no longer sustain the research and preserve the natural habitat for butterflies.

items.title: Trump pushes voting changes that Congress may deliver
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: With control of the White House and Congress, Republicans are pushing for major voting changes like voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements, saying the measures are needed to restore public confidence. Democrats say the proposals would make it more difficult to vote and that the erosion in public trust stems from false claims by President-elect Donald Trump and his allies of voter fraud in the 2020 election.

items.title: AP Breakthrough Entertainer: Adria Arjona's confidence keeps growing
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: Adria Arjona's big year included roles in Richard Linklater's "Hit Man" opposite Glen Powell, "The Absence of Eden" alongside Zoe Saldaña — and Zoë Kravitz’s directorial debut "Blink Twice." She's now celebrating the release of "Los Frikis," which she also executive produced, and returns next year to the acclaimed Star Wars series "Andor." After being named one of AP's Breakthrough Entertainers of 2024, the 32-year-old star sat down with AP entertainment journalist Leslie Ambriz to talk about her journey into film and TV, her mother's on-set support, and boosting Latin American stories in Hollywood.  #adriaarjona #hollywood #apnews #andor

items.content:

items.title: Meet the camels that take part in Spain's festive Three Kings tradition
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
In many Spanish families, children receive presents from the Three Kings on Epiphany, Jan. 6, rather than from Santa on Christmas Day. The night before, towns across Spain celebrate with parades re-enacting the Magi’s journey to Bethlehem, when actors portraying the wise men are often accompanied by camels. The animals are a much-loved part of the tradition, and children often leave carrots out for them in their homes, just as they do for Santa's reindeer. But if camels make an appearance on Spanish streets just one day a year, where are they the rest of the time?

items.title: As flooding becomes a yearly disaster in South Sudan, thousands survive on the edge of a canal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Flooding is becoming a yearly disaster in South Sudan. Tens of thousands of South Sudanese perch in makeshift communities on the rare high ground of the Jonglei Canal. Seasonal flooding has long been part of the lifestyle of pastoral communities around the Sudd, the largest wetland in Africa. But the swamp keeps growing, submerging villages, ruining farmland and killing livestock. Residents describe wading for hours to the nearest clinic through waters where poisonous snakes are a risk. (AP video shot by: Florence Miettaux, production by Jackson Njehia)

items.content:

Particularly tense scenes on Thursday underscored the discontent that many residents of the archipelago in the Indian Ocean feel toward their government, based about 8,000 kilometers (5,000 miles) away in Paris. items.title: Separated for decades, Assad's fall spurs hope for families split by Golan Heights buffer zone
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

Some families in Majdal Shams, a mountain town populated by members of the Druze religious community, haven't seen their loved ones in decades. But the fall of Syrian President Bashar Assad has spurred hope that family meetings might be allowed. Read more: https://bit.ly/4gmCXKC
